claims.claimText: Rotator Cuff Tear pathoanatomy centers on rotator-cuff, supraspinatus, infraspinatus and related stabilizers.

metadata.claim_text: Rotator Cuff Tear pathoanatomy centers on rotator-cuff, supraspinatus, infraspinatus and related stabilizers.

claims.claimText: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating rotator cuff tear.

metadata.claim_text: Students often underweight exam findings and over-rely on imaging when evaluating rotator cuff tear.

claims.claimText: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for rotator cuff tear.

metadata.claim_text: State mechanism, key exam findings, imaging role, treatment concept, and return-to-play framework for rotator cuff tear.

claims.claimText: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for rotator cuff tear — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

metadata.claim_text: Do not anchor on a single exam maneuver for rotator cuff tear — integrate mechanism, instability symptoms, and imaging pattern.

claims.claimText: Rotator cuff tears are shoulder dysfunction problems where age, acuity, and functional loss shape urgency.

metadata.claim_text: Rotator cuff tears are shoulder dysfunction problems where age, acuity, and functional loss shape urgency.

claims.claimText: Weak active elevation with preserved passive ROM suggests cuff tear rather than adhesive capsulitis.

metadata.claim_text: Weak active elevation with preserved passive ROM suggests cuff tear rather than adhesive capsulitis.

claims.claimText: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for rotator cuff tear require attending-defined progression gates.

metadata.claim_text: Rehabilitation milestones and return-to-play criteria for rotator cuff tear require attending-defined progression gates.

decisionPoints.trigger: Active athlete with functional instability or mechanical symptoms from rotator cuff tear despite appropriate initial management
decisionPoints.action: Discuss operative reconstruction versus continued rehabilitation; graft selection and fixation are attending decisions

decisionPoints.trigger: Athlete approaching return-to-sport after rotator cuff tear treatment
decisionPoints.action: Apply sport-specific return-to-play criteria with strength, stability, and functional testing milestones
